compiled_state.title: As a plugin maintainer, I have a versioned event schema and emission helper for review events
compiled_state.author: Main
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: A versioned JSONL event schema contract document and a shell script (emit-review-event.sh) for writing review events to the tickets orphan branch
**Why**: All instrumentation stories depend on a shared schema and reliable write mechanism. This is the foundation of the observability pipeline.
**Scope**:
- IN: Schema contract document with version field, event types (review_result, commit_workflow, tier_selection, overlay_trigger), field definitions; emit-review-event.sh with unique filenames (timestamp-random suffix); graceful failure handling
- OUT: Reporting logic, specific workflow instrumentation, compaction

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, a contract document defines the JSONL event schema with a version field and field definitions for all event types
  <- Satisfies: 'A versioned event schema contract document defines the JSONL field structure'
- When this story is complete, emit-review-event.sh writes a single JSONL event to the orphan branch with a unique filename and handles write failures gracefully
  <- Satisfies: 'Concurrent event writes use per-agent unique filenames'
- When this story is complete, unit tests verify valid JSON output, unique filenames, and graceful failure handling
  <- Satisfies: unit test DoD

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Schema contract is load-bearing — all instrumentation points reference it; design for forward compatibility
- [Architecture] emit-review-event.sh must reuse ticket-lib.sh orphan branch write infrastructure (flock, gc guard, atomic staging, push retry) rather than reimplementing — avoid divergent write paths
- [Security] Events must contain counts and severities only — no finding text or code snippets

comments.body: Story complete: all 5 tasks closed, completion verifier PASS
comments.author: Main
